name: Country-Fried Pork and Peppers
recipeIngredient: 4 Chef's Prime Filet (3/4-inch-thick boneless pork rib end steak)
1 tablespoon whole wheat flour or 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour
1 medium green pepper, cut in strips
1 medium sweet red pepper, cut in strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound pork to 1/4-inch thickness.
2. Stir together the cornmeal, flour, paprika, garlic salt, onion powder, pepper and cumin.
3. Dip pork in water; allow excess water to drip off.
4. Coat with cornmeal mixture on both sides.
5. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
6. Cook pork for 4 minutes, turning once.
7. Add the pepper strips and cook for 4 more minutes or until the pork is nicely browned and just slightly pink in the center.
8. Remove pork from the skillet and let it rest for a moment.
9. Cook peppers 1-2 minutes longer if necessary until tender.
10. Serve pork with peppers.
Source:
"National Pork Producers Council"
http://www.nppc.org
